entries.title: Campus Overview, Map, and Current Names
sampleQuestions: Where can I find a campus map?
Is the North Building the Main Building?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe is at 2601 24th Ave SE, Norman, OK 73071.
The main public areas are the Main Building, Children's Center, The Pointe, Care Center, and CARS Ministry garage.
Main Building is the preferred name for the North Building shown on the older map.
Sanctuary and Auditorium refer to the same main worship room, with Sanctuary preferred.
Student Center is the preferred name for Student Sanctuary on the older map.
Children's Center is the physical area; Kingdom Kids is the ministry.
Community Center refers to the gymnasium and weight-room side of The Pointe.

entries.title: Welcome Center, Great Hall, Sanctuary, Event Hall, and Offices
sampleQuestions: How do I find the Sanctuary?
How do I get to the church office?
Where can I get regular coffee?
requiredFacts: Guest parking near the Welcome Center is marked by feather flags, and the Welcome Center desk is immediately visible after entering the Great Hall.
The Great Hall is the Main Building's large lobby and contains the Welcome Center and standard Sunday coffee.
From the Welcome Center entrance, turn left into the hallway for the Sanctuary.
The first-floor Event Hall is straight down the Great Hall and entered from it.
During Sunday services, reach the offices by walking about halfway down the Great Hall, turning right, continuing to the end of that hallway, and turning right again.
During Monday-through-Thursday office hours, public visitors may use the separate office entrance.
requiredActions: During services, recommend the Welcome Center for in-person navigation help.

entries.title: Children's Center, Family Check-In, Nursery, and The Palace
sampleQuestions: Where do I check my kids in?
Which entrance should a first-time family use?
requiredFacts: The Children's Center entrance is the recommended Sunday entrance for first-time families and has nearby guest and accessible parking on the north side of the parking lot.
From the main Welcome Center, continue straight down the Great Hall, cross the actual castle-themed drawbridge, continue to the end, and find Children's check-in on the right.
Nursery and preschool are on the first floor.
The breastfeeding room is across the hall from the nursery.
The Palace is the upstairs elementary area and is served by the Children's Center elevator and stairs.
The outdoor playground has an exterior gate.
prohibitedClaims: Do not provide child-security workarounds, gate-access details, or supervision procedures.

entries.title: Adult Classrooms, Elevators, and Main Building Restrooms
requiredFacts: Adult Classrooms are individually numbered and upstairs in a separate area from the upstairs Children's Center.
The elevator directly to the right after entering the Great Hall serves Adult Classrooms.
The Children's Center elevator serves The Palace and upstairs Children's Center.
Signs upstairs direct visitors to individual Adult Classroom numbers.
Public restrooms are in the hallway outside the Sanctuary, the hallway to the offices, and the Children's Center, and are clearly marked.
requiredActions: For an unknown classroom number, recommend following on-site signs or asking at the Welcome Center rather than inventing directions.
prohibitedClaims: Do not assume someone can use stairs; provide the correct elevator route when accessibility is relevant.

entries.title: The Pointe, Pointe Brew, Student Center, Classrooms, and Gym
sampleQuestions: How do I find Pointe Brew?
How do I get to the gym?
requiredFacts: The Pointe is on the south side of campus and requires an outdoor walk from the Main Building.
The main Pointe entrance opens into the shared lobby, with Student check-in immediately inside, Pointe Brew directly in the lobby, and the Student Center connected to the lobby.
The Pointe classrooms are in back: go through the Game Room, then the double set of glass doors into the classroom hallway.
The gym may be reached past the Student Center through the connecting hallway and gym door, or from the door at the end of the classroom hallway.
Community Center refers to the gymnasium and weight-room side of The Pointe.

entries.title: Care Center and CARS Ministry Locations
sampleQuestions: Which entrance do I use for the food pantry?
Should I go to the CARS garage without an appointment?
requiredFacts: The Care Center is a separate building not internally connected to the Main Building; visitors should park in the large lot between the Main Building and The Pointe and use the clearly labeled Care Center entrance.
The CARS Ministry garage is a separate building east of The Pointe.
Someone with a confirmed CARS appointment may go to the garage and should follow CARS arrival instructions.
Someone without an appointment should use the official application instead of arriving at the garage.

entries.title: Guest Parking and Accessible Routes
sampleQuestions: Which entrance should I use with a wheelchair?
requiredFacts: The campus is wheelchair accessible and ADA compliant.
All mapped public entrances are wheelchair accessible except the separate exterior Pointe Brew entrance under the Pointe Brew sign.
Use the main Pointe entrance for an accessible route to Pointe Brew.
Accessible parking is near the north entrance to the Sanctuary and in the Children's Center parking area.
Guest parking is near both the Welcome Center entrance and Children's Center entrance.
requiredActions: Use the correct elevator and avoid presenting stairs as the only route.
prohibitedClaims: Do not promise a particular parking space or claim an unknown temporary obstruction or elevator outage does not exist.

entries.title: Navigation Help and Facility Safety Boundaries
sampleQuestions: Can you track where I am?
Which locked door should I use?
How do I get into a restricted room?
requiredFacts: During Sunday services, the Welcome Center can help people find public rooms; at other times, the church office is the approved contact.
requiredActions: Ask for the person's current public landmark and destination only when needed for clearer directions.
prohibitedClaims: Do not request or track live personal location data.

Do not provide keys, codes, credentials, access-control instructions, security positions, staff-only routes, infrastructure details, child-security workarounds, or instructions for entering locked areas.
If a route is unavailable, direct the person to the Welcome Center or office rather than suggesting unauthorized access.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office
